channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇨🇺⚖️ US indicts former Cuban leader Raúl Castro over 1996 shootdown of civilian planes
item.description: Federal prosecutors charged Castro with murder and conspiracy for ordering Cuban jets to down two civilian aircraft flown by Miami-based exiles, killing four people including three Americans. The case escalates US pressure on Cuba's communist government amid an ongoing oil blockade.

item.title: 🇨🇩🦠 Rare Ebola strain spreads rapidly in eastern Congo as health workers struggle
item.description: The Bundibugyo virus, a rare type of Ebola, has spread undetected for weeks in eastern Congo, with 139 suspected deaths and 600 cases. WHO says cases may exceed 1,000, with a vaccine months away amid conflict zones and underfunded health systems.

item.title: 🇮🇱⚓ Far-right Israeli minister Ben-Gvir condemned for taunting handcuffed Gaza flotilla activists
item.description: National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir posted video showing himself telling detained pro-Palestinian activists "We are the masters" while they knelt bound. The footage drew condemnation from the US, UK, Canada, France, Italy, Australia and others, with Netanyahu calling it "not in line with Israel's values."

item.title: 🇺🇸🔍 US military surveillance flights track near Cuba as tensions mount
item.description: BBC Verify analysis shows at least five US Navy P-8A Poseidon jets and three MQ-4C Triton drones have operated near Cuba since May 11, with flights as close as 50 miles from the island. Experts say the visible flight transponders are likely a deliberate signal to enforce the oil blockade and deter Venezuela.

item.title: 🇦🇹🕵️ Austrian ex-intelligence officer convicted of spying for Russia
item.description: Egisto Ott, 63, was sentenced to four years for passing classified information to Russian intelligence and Wirecard fugitive Jan Marsalek. The trial revealed Ott copied phones from Austrian interior ministry officials after they fell into the Danube, reviving concerns about Russian espionage in Austria.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺 Xi and Putin Conclude Beijing Summit Without Finalizing Gas Pipeline Deal
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin oversaw 40+ cooperation agreements in Beijing, emphasizing energy trade. Russia's oil exports to China grew 35% in Q1 2026, though the stalled Power of Siberia 2 gas pipeline remains unresolved.

item.title: 🇨🇺 US Expected to Unveil Criminal Charges Against Cuba's Raúl Castro
item.description: Federal prosecutors are preparing charges against the former Cuban president over the 1996 downing of two civilian aircraft operated by the Miami-based Brothers to the Rescue group. The move escalates US pressure on Cuba's communist leadership.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump-Xi China Summit: Trade, Iran, Taiwan on Agenda
item.description: US President Donald Trump met with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing for high-stakes talks covering trade, the Iran conflict, and Taiwan. The visit comes amid heightened tensions over US-China relations.

item.title: 🇮🇱⚔️ Hezbollah Claims 24 Operations Against Israeli Forces
item.description: Hezbollah announced it carried out 24 operations targeting Israeli forces amid ongoing regional tensions. The group continues to maintain pressure along Israel's northern border.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇷🇺 Merz: Putin Boosts Russia's Far-Right Ties in Europe
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz highlighted Vladimir Putin's ongoing efforts to cultivate relationships with far-right political movements across Europe, warning of Russian influence operations.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia's Arafura Rare Earth Project to Start Construction in September
item.description: Australia's Arafura Resources announced its rare earth processing project will begin construction in September, part of efforts to diversify critical mineral supply chains away from China.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australian Court Upholds $465,000 Fine Against Elon Musk's X
item.description: An Australian court upheld a fine against X Corp for failing to disclose information about child safety measures, marking another regulatory challenge for Elon Musk's social media platform.

item.title: 🇺🇸⛽ US Drivers Face Gasoline Price Shocks This Summer Travel Season
item.description: Reuters reports that US drivers may face additional gasoline price shocks during the summer travel season, with supply constraints and geopolitical factors driving uncertainty.

item.title: 🇺🇸💊 US Working with Small Biotech Firm on Experimental Ebola Treatment
item.description: The US government is collaborating with a small biotech company to develop an experimental treatment for Ebola amid the ongoing outbreak in Congo caused by the rare Bundibugyo strain.

item.title: 🇬🇧💷 UK Inflation Cools to 2.8% in April
item.description: UK inflation slowed to 2.8% in April, bringing relief to the Bank of England as it considers monetary policy adjustments. Sterling dipped following the data release.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 NATO's Rutte: US Military Presence in Europe Critical
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte emphasized the critical importance of continued US military presence in Europe amid concerns about transatlantic defense commitments.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇹🇼 Trump to Speak with Taiwan's President
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced he would speak with Taiwan's president, a move that could affect US-China relations and cross-strait dynamics.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan Leader: Foreign Forces Cannot Decide Island's Future
item.description: Taiwan's president stated that foreign forces cannot determine the island's future, reaffirming Taiwan's sovereignty stance amid pressure from Beijing.

item.title: 🇳🇴🛢️ Norway's Offshore Oil Production Beats Forecasts in April
item.description: Norway's offshore oil production exceeded expectations in April, providing some relief to European energy markets amid global supply concerns.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Leaves OPEC: Implications for Global Oil Markets
item.description: The UAE's departure from OPEC raises questions about global oil supply dynamics and cartel influence, with potential implications for prices and market stability.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚠️ Iran Warns Renewed US-Israel Attack Could Expand Conflict Beyond Region
item.description: Iran warned that any renewed military action by the US and Israel could escalate the conflict beyond the Middle East region, amid ongoing tensions following ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Pentagon Officials: Beijing Visit Doubt Over $14 Billion Taiwan Arms Package
item.description: Reuters reports that Pentagon officials' Beijing visit casts doubt on a $14 billion US arms package for Taiwan, highlighting the complex dynamics of US-China-Taiwan relations.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇨🇳 Xi-Putin Summit: Nuclear Energy, Taiwan, and Trump's Golden Dome Key Points
item.description: The Beijing summit between Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin covered nuclear energy cooperation, Taiwan discussions, and responses to Trump's Golden Dome initiative.

item.title: 🇬🇧📊 UK Inflation Slows to 2.8% in April
item.description: UK inflation cooled to 2.8% in April, providing relief to consumers and the Bank of England. Sterling dipped following the data release as markets assessed monetary policy implications.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 China's Xi May Visit North Korea Early Next Week
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping may visit North Korea early next week, according to Yonhap news agency, in what would be a significant diplomatic move amid regional tensions.
